title: AgentOwnershipContract
description: Agent Ownership Framework (AOF) v1 contract. Declares the purpose, ownership, authority, data access, incident response, governance, lifecycle, compliance, risk, signoff, dependencies, and SLA for a production AI agent. Covers all 8 agent governance boundaries.

apiVersion.description: The AOF API version this contract conforms to.

kind.description: The resource kind. Must be AgentOwnershipContract.

metadata.description: Contract document metadata. Identifies and versions the contract itself.

name.description: Unique slug identifying this contract. Must be kebab-case.

version.description: Semantic version of this contract document (not the agent software version).

created.description: ISO 8601 date when this contract was first created.

updated.description: ISO 8601 date when this contract was last updated.

labels.description: Key-value string pairs for categorization, filtering, and tooling integration.

annotations.description: Key-value string pairs for supplementary non-identifying metadata (e.g., ticket URLs, Confluence links, runbook URLs).

agent.description: Describes the AI agent: its identity, classification, and domain.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for the agent. Used in logs, alerts, and cross-references. Must be kebab-case.

name.description: Human-readable display name for the agent.

description.description: Clear description of the agent's behavior and scope. Minimum 50 characters.

type.description: Operational classification of the agent. autonomous=acts without human triggers; hybrid=triggered by humans but acts autonomously; advisory=provides recommendations only.

domain.description: Business domain the agent operates in (e.g., financial-services, customer-service, operations).

tags.description: Free-form tags for search and categorization.

purpose.description: Boundary 1 — Purpose. Declares the business purpose, intended outcomes, success criteria, and explicit out-of-scope behaviors for this agent.

business_purpose.description: The business problem this agent solves and why it exists. Must be specific enough to distinguish it from adjacent agents. Minimum 50 characters.

success_metrics.description: Key measurable outcomes that define whether this agent is succeeding. Key = metric name, value = target or description.

out_of_scope.description: Explicit list of things this agent is NOT intended to do. This is as important as what it does — it prevents scope creep and clarifies system boundaries.

ownership.description: Boundary 2 — Ownership. Declares four named governance roles: domain owner (business accountability), technical owner (build and runtime), data owner (data quality and compliance), and risk owner (regulatory exposure). domain_owner and technical_owner are required.

domain_owner.description: Business outcome owner. Accountable for acceptable use, business risk if the agent fails, and defining what success looks like. Must be a named person, not a team alias.

technical_owner.description: Engineering accountability owner. Accountable for build quality, runtime behavior, deployment decisions, and operational runbooks.

data_owner.description: Optional. Accountable for data quality, compliance with data governance policy, retention schedule, and data access decisions.

risk_owner.description: Optional. Accountable for regulatory exposure assessment, audit readiness, and communicating risk posture to compliance and legal.

escalation_path.description: Ordered sequence of escalation contacts. Level 1 is first point of escalation beyond the named owners.

level.description: Escalation level. Must be sequential starting at 1.

name.description: Full name of the escalation contact.

email.description: Email address of the escalation contact.

role.description: Title or role of the escalation contact.

authority.description: Boundary 3 — Authority. Declares what the agent can decide autonomously, what triggers escalation, what it can never do, and who can stop it.

autonomous_decisions.description: List of decisions this agent is authorized to make without human approval. Each entry must describe the decision type, any limits, and the rationale.

decision_type.description: Short label for this type of autonomous decision (e.g., 'issue_refund', 'route_transaction', 'send_notification').

limit.description: Optional numeric cap for this decision (e.g., 500 for a $500 refund limit).

currency.description: Optional currency code if limit is a monetary cap (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

requires_approval.description: Whether this decision requires human approval before execution. If true, describe the approval mechanism in the description field.

description.description: Full description of what this decision covers, any conditions or constraints, and why autonomous authority is appropriate.

escalation_triggers.description: Conditions that require the agent to stop and escalate to a human rather than proceeding autonomously. Minimum 1 item.

prohibited_actions.description: Things this agent can NEVER do under any circumstances. These are hard limits, not soft guidelines. Minimum 1 item.

override_mechanism.description: Defines who can pause or override this agent and how fast they can act.

who_can_pause.description: Named roles authorized to pause this agent. Each entry must specify the role, the method (how they pause it), and the SLA in minutes.

role.description: Role or title of the person who can pause this agent.

method.description: The mechanism for pausing (e.g., 'feature flag in LaunchDarkly', 'kill switch in ops console', 'PagerDuty escalation').

sla_minutes.description: Maximum time in minutes from decision-to-pause to agent being fully stopped.

who_can_override.description: Named role that can override any agent decision or override the pause/resume decision itself (e.g., 'Chief Risk Officer', 'VP Engineering').

data.description: Boundary 4 — Data. Declares what data sources the agent can access, what is explicitly prohibited, and how sensitive data must be handled.

permitted_sources.description: Data sources this agent is explicitly authorized to access. If a source is not listed here, access is not permitted.

source.description: Name of the data source (e.g., 'Customer CRM', 'Transaction History DB', 'Fraud Score Ledger').

fields.description: Optional list of specific fields or tables the agent can access within this source. If omitted, all fields in the source are permitted.

access_level.description: Whether the agent can read only or also write to this source.

time_window_days.description: Optional: how many days of historical data the agent is permitted to access.

reason.description: Optional: why access to this source is needed for the agent's purpose.

prohibited_sources.description: Data sources this agent is explicitly NOT permitted to access. Enumerating prohibitions makes unauthorized access easier to detect.

sensitive_data_handling.description: Rules for handling sensitive or personal data. All rules are binding on all environments including dev/staging.

must_mask.description: PII or sensitive fields that must be masked in all logs, traces, and outputs. These fields must never appear in plain text in any audit or debugging context.

must_encrypt_in_transit.description: All data in transit between this agent and any data source or downstream system must be encrypted (TLS 1.2+ minimum).

cannot_retain.description: If true, this agent cannot store sensitive data beyond the duration of the active request/session. No caching, no persistent local state.

retention_policy.description: Defines how long data processed by this agent may be retained, and under what authority. Must reference a regulatory or policy basis. Minimum 20 characters.

incident_response.description: Boundaries 5 and 8 — Evidence and Failure Accountability. Declares who investigates failures, what the investigation must cover, and who owns communication to customers, stakeholders, and regulators.

investigator_primary.description: Role name of the primary investigator for any production incident (e.g., 'technical_owner', 'Senior Engineer On-Call'). This person leads root cause analysis and log access.

investigator_secondary.description: Role name of the secondary investigator — takes over if primary is unavailable, or co-investigates for critical-tier incidents.

log_access.description: Optional: named roles that have access to agent logs and traces for investigation purposes. If omitted, access is governed by the organization's default log access policy.

investigation_scope.description: Mandatory questions the investigation must answer. Minimum 3 items. These define the minimum bar for incident reconstruction.

communication.description: Defines who owns external and internal communication during and after an incident.

customer_owner.description: Role responsible for communicating with affected customers during and after an incident.

stakeholder_owner.description: Role responsible for communicating with internal stakeholders (executives, product, business units).

compliance_owner.description: Role responsible for communicating with regulators, auditors, and legal counsel. This person determines whether regulatory disclosure is required.

post_incident_review.description: Requirements for post-incident reviews (post-mortems). Required for any agent with risk_tier medium or above.

required.description: Whether a post-incident review document must be produced after every production incident.

sla_days.description: Number of days after incident resolution by which the post-incident review must be completed and published.

participants.description: Roles that must participate in the post-incident review.

governance.description: Controls review cadence, change approval requirements, and the governing body.

review_cadence.description: How often the contract and agent behavior must be formally reviewed.

approval_required_for.description: List of change types that require explicit governance board approval before implementation.

change_control_board.description: Name or contact of the change control board or governance committee for this agent.

last_reviewed.description: ISO 8601 date of the most recent governance review.

next_review.description: ISO 8601 date of the next scheduled governance review.

annotations.description: Optional key-value annotations for governance metadata (e.g., runbook URL, on-call schedule link).

lifecycle.description: Boundary 6 — Lifecycle. Tracks the deployment status, versioning policy, monitoring expectations, and retirement criteria for this agent.

status.description: Current lifecycle status. active=in production; conditional=limited rollout; pilot=experimental; paused=temporarily suspended; retired=decommissioned.

deployed_at.description: ISO 8601 date when the agent was first deployed to production.

deprecated_at.description: ISO 8601 date when this agent was marked deprecated (still running but no new traffic).

retirement_date.description: ISO 8601 date when this agent will be fully decommissioned.

replacement_agent_id.description: The agent.id of the agent that replaces this one (if deprecated or retired).

versioning.description: Optional. Defines the change approval policy and what constitutes a material change requiring a governance review.

all_changes_require_approval.description: If true, any change to the agent or its contract requires explicit governance approval before deployment.

material_change_definition.description: Conditions that constitute a material change, triggering a new review cycle and signoff requirement.

monitoring.description: Optional. Documents what is being monitored in production and how frequently the monitoring outputs are reviewed.

real_time_metrics.description: Metrics being monitored in real time in production (e.g., error rate, latency p99, decision distribution).

review_frequency_days.description: How often (in days) monitoring outputs are formally reviewed by the technical owner.

retirement.description: Optional. Defines the conditions that trigger decommissioning and the planned review date.

criteria.description: Conditions that would trigger decommissioning of this agent (e.g., replacement agent is stable, regulatory requirement removed).

sunset_date.description: Hard date after which this agent must be retired, regardless of replacement status.

planned_review_date.description: Date when the retirement decision will be formally reviewed.

compliance.description: Regulatory frameworks, data classification, PII handling policy, and audit requirements.

frameworks.description: Regulatory or compliance frameworks this agent must satisfy.

data_classification.description: Sensitivity level of data the agent accesses or produces. public=no restrictions; internal=employee access only; restricted=limited access with controls; highly-restricted=strongest controls, explicit authorization required.

pii_handling.description: Plain-language description of how personally identifiable information is processed, stored, and protected.

audit_log_required.description: Whether all agent inputs, outputs, and decisions must be written to an immutable audit log.

retention_policy.description: Data retention requirements for agent inputs, outputs, logs, and decisions.

risk.description: Risk profile of the agent: tier, blast radius, human oversight requirements, and kill switch.

risk_tier.description: Risk classification. low=minimal impact if it fails; medium=significant but contained; high=major business or customer impact; critical=existential or regulatory risk.

blast_radius.description: Plain-language description of the worst-case impact if this agent fails, is compromised, or behaves incorrectly. Must be specific and honest.

human_in_the_loop_required.description: Whether a human must review and approve consequential actions before the agent executes them.

kill_switch_owner.description: Email of the named individual authorized to immediately disable this agent in production.

signoff.description: Boundary 7 — Approval. Named signatures from each owner role confirming they have reviewed this contract and accept accountability before the agent launches.

domain_owner.description: Sign-off from the domain owner. Required before launch. Confirms business purpose, out-of-scope boundaries, and acceptable use have been reviewed.

technical_owner.description: Sign-off from the technical owner. Required before launch. Confirms build quality, authority limits, data access, and incident response are correctly declared.

data_owner.description: Optional sign-off from the data owner. Required if a data_owner is assigned in the ownership section.

risk_owner.description: Optional sign-off from the risk owner. Required if a risk_owner is assigned in the ownership section.

dependencies.description: External models, services, and data sources this agent depends on.

models.description: LLMs or ML models used by the agent.

services.description: Internal or external services the agent calls.

data_sources.description: Databases, event streams, or datasets the agent reads from.

sla.description: Service level agreement for the agent's production operation.

availability.description: Target availability as a percentage (0-100). Example: 99.9 means 99.9% uptime.

max_latency_ms.description: Maximum acceptable end-to-end latency in milliseconds.

error_rate_threshold.description: Maximum acceptable error rate as a human-readable string (e.g., '0.1%', '< 1 per 1000 requests').

throughput_limit.description: Maximum request throughput as a human-readable string (e.g., '500 req/min', '10000 req/hour').

named_owner.description: A named individual in a specific governance role for this agent.

role.description: Job title or governance role of this owner (e.g., 'Director, Customer Support', 'Staff Engineer').

email.description: Work email address of the owner. Must be a real individual's email, not a team alias.

accountability.description: Explicit list of what this owner is responsible for. Be specific — this defines what they are signing off on.

signoff_entry.description: A named signature from an owner role confirming they have reviewed and accepted accountability.

name.description: Full name of the person signing off.

confirmation.description: The statement this person is confirming by signing. Should be specific to their role's accountability.

dependency.description: An external system, model, or data source this agent depends on.

type.description: Category of dependency (e.g., llm, ml-model, rest-api, postgresql, kafka, s3).

description.description: Brief description of what this dependency is used for.
